codeReview.summary: The feature-specific follow-up commit only adds focused Rust regression coverage for lifecycle label classification. The tests align with the feature contract by asserting one authoritative primary user window across repeated opens and by excluding support/prewarm labels from user-window identity. I did not find blocking or non-blocking defects in this diff.

sharedStateObservations.observation: Validation-mode startup can still fail in the renderer before real desktop lifecycle evidence is collected because @tauri-apps/plugin-os reads platform from an undefined Tauri context. This blocker is mentioned in the handoff and visible in .tmp/tauri-dev.err.log, but the durable shared-state docs only mention checking those logs when bun tauri dev --no-watch exits early; they do not capture the concrete plugin-os failure mode that later workers repeatedly hit.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: C:\Users\admin\tauri-react\.tmp\tauri-dev.err.log:1 shows Uncaught TypeError: Cannot read properties of undefined (reading platform) at node_modules\@tauri-apps\plugin-os\dist-js\index.js:35:0; handoff for worker session 74d91495-95f3-4b1b-94b6-284227f591a1 records the same issue as a discoveredIssue. C:\Users\admin\tauri-react\.factory\library\user-testing.md documents the log files to inspect but not this recurring root cause.

summary: Reviewed mission context, the lifecycle-foundation handoff/transcript skeleton for worker session 74d91495-95f3-4b1b-94b6-284227f591a1, and commit 9e31383. The diff is a narrow regression-test hardening change in src-tauri/src/utils/window.rs and correctly reinforces the lifecycle identity invariants without introducing review issues. One shared-state knowledge gap remains: the recurring @tauri-apps/plugin-os startup failure is evidenced in logs and handoff notes but is not yet captured in the mission library.
